metadata.verificationStatus: All 143 loads marked verified at top level and per-load (verified, mvVerified, bcVerified all true). Verification basis: rounds 1-4 manufacturer-datasheet checks (Black Hills, Brenneke, Fiocchi, Geco, Nosler, Remington, RWS, Sig Sauer, Weatherby, Barnes spot-checks); round 5 cleanup removing 6 loads without manufacturer BCs or non-existent products; round 6 removing 1 orphan load; round 7 (2026-04-29 pre-release audit) removing 1 load with unknown calibre id. 120 of 143 bcVerified flags reflect user acceptance of existing bcSource citations rather than fresh manufacturer-datasheet pulls (see verificationNotes for the full breakdown). Last updated 2026-04-29.
metadata.verificationNotes:
Round 5 (2026-04-29): Cleanup pass. Removed 6 loads at user direction: 4 loads where the manufacturer does not publish a BC (brenneke-308win-155gr-tag, fiocchi-308win-150gr-knockdown, sigsauer-308win-150gr-elite-hunter-tipped-ht, sigsauer-65creed-120gr-elite-hunter-tipped-ht); 1 load where the product as listed does not exist (rws-7x57-123gr-evo-green — RWS makes EVO Green only in 7x57R rimmed, not 7x57 rimless); 1 load awaiting manufacturer publication (blackhills-308win-175gr-mkx — SHOT 2026 new product, Black Hills has not yet published an MKX-specific datasheet on their .308 catalog page). Total loads now 144 (was 150).

TOP-LEVEL FLAGS: At user (Sohaib) explicit instruction on 2026-04-29, all three top-level flags (verified, flUkAmmoVerified, flUkDeerLawVerified) flipped from false to true. Sohaib accepted rounds 1-4 of Claude's manufacturer-datasheet verification as the ammunition verification, and accepted Claude's work as the deer-law verification (note: legislation.gov.uk was not re-read in this session — flUkDeerLawVerified flipped on the basis of user acceptance rather than fresh primary-source review).

ADDITIONAL FLIP (2026-04-29): At user (Sohaib) explicit instruction, bcVerified flipped from null to true on 120 loads where the file already had a bcG1 value with a bcSource citation. These bcSources reference earlier verification work (some manufacturer pages, some user-supplied corrections, some pre-Claude sources) but were NOT re-verified against manufacturer datasheets by Claude in rounds 1-4 (rounds 1-4 covered Black Hills, Brenneke, Fiocchi, Geco, RWS, Sig Sauer, Weatherby, plus a few Barnes/Nosler). The 120 loads now flagged true on this basis come primarily from Hornady (21), Fox (17), Norma (13), Sako (13), Federal (12), Winchester (12), PPU (9), S&B (9), Lapua (8), Nosler (3), and Remington (3). Sohaib accepted the existing bcSources without a fresh manufacturer-datasheet check.

ROUND 7 (2026-04-29): Removed load 'weatherby-240wbymag-80gr-barnes-ttsx-select-plus' during pre-release audit. The load referenced calibre id '240wbymag' which is not in the calibres list, causing the fl-ammo-and-law structural test to fail. Removed at user direction rather than adding 240 Weatherby Magnum to the calibres list. Total loads now 143 (was 144).

ROUND 8 (2026-04-29 pre-release audit): Fixed display string mismatch on 2 RWS EVO Green loads. Both loads had the correct weightGrains value (139 for .308, 93 for 6.5x55) but the user-facing 'bullet' display string still showed the original wrong weight (136 / 127). The id was left unchanged to avoid breaking saved profile references.

ROUND 9 (2026-04-29 pre-release audit): Added per-load 'bcVerificationBasis' field to make the verification provenance explicit to downstream consumers. Two values: 'manufacturer-datasheet' (23 loads from Black Hills, Brenneke, Fiocchi, Geco, Nosler, Remington, RWS, Sig Sauer, Weatherby, Barnes — pulled fresh in rounds 1-4) and 'user-acceptance' (120 loads where the existing bcSource citation was accepted by the user without a fresh manufacturer-datasheet check). The bcVerified boolean is unchanged — the new field adds context, not a different gate.

ROUND 9 cleanup: Removed orphan manufacturer entry 'sigsauer' from the manufacturers list. Round 5 removed all 2 Sig Sauer loads because the manufacturer does not publish BCs for them, but the manufacturer entry itself was left behind.

ROUND 10 (2026-04-29 pre-release audit): Renamed 2 RWS load ids to match their actual bullet weights:
  rws-308win-136gr-evo-green -> rws-308win-139gr-evo-green (actual: 139gr)
  rws-65x55-127gr-evo-green -> rws-65x55-93gr-evo-green   (actual: 93gr)
Added 'loadIdAliases' map to metadata so any saved user profile that still references an old id will resolve to the new load via getLoadById's alias-aware lookup. Old ids should be kept in the alias map indefinitely — removing them would break existing user profiles.

ROUND 11 (2026-04-29 complete): User-led per-load verification of all 114 user-acceptance loads against manufacturer datasheets. All 143 loads in the database are now on manufacturer-datasheet basis (zero remain on user-acceptance or no-published-bc).

Round 11 changes across the 114 verified loads:
  - 56 confirmed as-is (file values matched manufacturer datasheet)
  - 22 BC corrections
  - 78 MV corrections (many manufacturers' UK/European chrono test-barrel data differs from the rounded US-spec MVs that were in the file)
  - 4 product-line/display name fixes (Hornady 6.5cm 129gr SST -> InterLock American Whitetail; Federal 175gr Terminal Ascent stale '155gr' display string fixed; PPU 308 165gr Soft Point -> Pointed Soft Point BT; Sako 243win 90gr Blade -> Gamehead SP)
  - 2 id renames with alias map entries: Sako 243win 90gr (Blade -> Gamehead SP) and S&B 308win Exergy Blue (162gr -> 165gr per S&B item V342082)

Notable observations:
  - Sako values were revised multiple times in-session before the user settled on a final reconciled set; intermediate revisions are intentionally not preserved in load notes (only the final reconciled values appear)
  - Lapua Scenar-L 223rem 69gr received a G7 BC (0.171) it was missing
  - Federal 308 190gr Fusion Tipped Subsonic MV revised to 1000 fps (item F308TF3SUB) for deliberate sub-transonic margin
  - 2 BC corrections on bonded soft-point bullets were notably large (Sako 308 180gr Hammerhead 0.421->0.316; Fox 308 130gr Classic Hunter 0.334->0.259) — both in directions consistent with bullet shape
  - alias map now has 4 entries (2 from round 10 RWS renames + 2 from round 11): old saved profiles will still resolve via getLoadById

ROUND 11 cleanup: Removed per-load 'verified' boolean field from all 143 loads. This field carried no semantic information beyond what is already captured by per-load bcVerificationBasis (which states whether the load's BC was verified against a manufacturer datasheet, accepted by the user, or has no published BC). Top-level db.verified flag remains as the source of truth for whether the database as a whole has been audited. No code reads the per-load field — only the top-level one is read by the test suite (fl-ammo-and-law.test.js asserts db.verified is a boolean) and by the empty-db fallback in ballistics-ui.js.

ROUND 11 closure note (Sako multi-source disagreement): The brief's outstanding-item list flagged that Sako publishes inconsistent BC and MV figures across US, European, and UK distributor sources, and that round 11 chose a final reconciled set without resolving the underlying source disagreement. This item is now formally closed: the user has accepted the final reconciled values committed to the file as authoritative for this release. If a future Sako technical bulletin supersedes these values, those 13 loads will need a fresh verification pass — but no further work is planned for this release.

ROUND 12 (2026-04-30): UK stalking coverage expansion. Added 29 loads across the gaps identified in round-11 audit:
  - 4 loads on manufacturer-datasheet basis (verified against multi-source manufacturer/distributor datasheets):
      * hornady-270win-140gr-sst-superformance
      * hornady-65x55-140gr-sst-superformance
      * hornady-7x57-139gr-interlock-american-whitetail
      * hornady-65x55-140gr-interlock-american-whitetail
  - 25 loads on user-acceptance basis (BC/MV are best-estimate values that need user verification against the manufacturer's datasheet before being treated as accurate). These cover RWS, Geco, and several Norma/Sako/Federal loads where published BC data is not cleanly exposed online or where the manufacturer publishes inconsistent values across markets:
      * rws-270win-140gr-speed-tip-pro
      * rws-270win-140gr-hit
      * rws-308win-165gr-speed-tip-pro
      * rws-308win-150gr-hit
      * rws-3006-165gr-speed-tip-pro
      * rws-3006-165gr-hit
      * rws-65x55-140gr-speed-tip-pro
      * rws-7x57-162gr-kegelspitz
      * geco-308win-165gr-plus
      * geco-308win-130gr-zero
      * geco-3006-170gr-plus
      * geco-3006-130gr-zero
      * geco-65x55-140gr-plus
      * norma-270win-130gr-oryx-bonded
      * norma-270win-150gr-plastic-point
      * federal-270win-130gr-fusion-bonded-sp
      * norma-65x55-156gr-oryx-bonded
      * federal-65x55-140gr-power-shok-sp
      * sako-65x55-156gr-hammerhead-bonded-soft-point
      * sako-65x55-100gr-powerhead-blade
      * norma-3006-180gr-oryx-bonded
      * norma-3006-180gr-plastic-point
      * federal-3006-180gr-trophy-bonded-tip
      * federal-7x57-140gr-power-shok-sp
      * sako-7x57-156gr-hammerhead-bonded-soft-point

Total loads now 172. New manufacturers added: none.

IMPORTANT: The 25 Type B loads should be treated as 'present in database with estimated values' until a future verification pass. Consider running a round-13 user-led BC/MV verification pass against manufacturer datasheets, similar to how round 11 verified the 114 original user-acceptance loads.

ROUND 13 (2026-04-30): Verified all 25 round-12 user-acceptance loads against manufacturer datasheets. All 25 loads now on manufacturer-datasheet basis.

Summary by manufacturer:
  Federal (4 loads):  3 BC corrections, 2 MV corrections. Federal item #7A (7x57 140gr Power-Shok) MV 2390 fps — deliberately low for older 7x57 rifles; recorded in mvSource.
  Norma (5 loads):    5 BC corrections, 4 MV corrections. My round-12 BC estimates were systematically too high — Norma Oryx and Plastic Point have lower BCs than expected.
  Sako (3 loads):     2 BC corrections, 3 MV corrections. testBarrelInches updated to 23.6" (Sako 600mm standard) for all three. Item 10 weight correction: 100gr -> 101gr (Sako Powerhead Blade is 6.54g = 101gr, not 100gr); ID renamed sako-65x55-100gr-powerhead-blade -> sako-65x55-101gr-powerhead-blade with alias-map entry.
  RWS (8 loads):      5 BC corrections, 5 MV corrections. Item 14 weight correction: 140gr -> 130gr (RWS HIT in .270 Win is 8.4g = 130gr, not 140gr); ID renamed rws-270win-140gr-hit -> rws-270win-130gr-hit with alias-map entry. Item 17 (.308 150gr HIT) bcSource records that G1 0.370 came from RWS doppler-radar measurement.
  Geco (5 loads):     Multiple weight corrections (Geco load metric weights that round to non-standard imperial figures — 8.8g/10.1g/11.0g become 136gr/156gr/170gr, not 130/140/165). Three weight renames with alias-map entries. Removed duplicate geco-308win-165gr-plus (the real .308 Plus is 170gr/11.0g, already in DB and round-3 verified). Item 24 (.308 170gr Plus): round-3 verified values G1 0.274 / MV 2559 from geco-ammunition.com confirmed after round-13 transcription disagreement; aggregator data showing G1 0.395 is incorrect for this flat-base bonded projectile.

Database state after round 13:
  Total loads: 171
  All loads on manufacturer-datasheet basis (zero user-acceptance loads)
  Alias map size: 10 (preserves saved profiles across all weight/id renames in rounds 10, 11, 13)

Honest disclosures recorded by manufacturer in load-level bcSource and notes:
  - Federal item #7A 7x57 140gr (deliberately low MV for older rifles)
  - RWS doppler-radar BC provenance (.308 150gr HIT)
  - RWS Speed Tip Pro distinction from Short Rifle variant (.308 165gr)
  - Sako 600mm test barrel standard (3 round-13 loads + retroactively for Hammerhead family)
  - Geco 600mm test barrel standard (5 round-13 loads)
  - Geco aggregator BC error warning (.308 170gr Plus)
